channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: 🔐 AES-256 Finally Makes Sense (And It’s Way Simpler Than You Think)
item.description: Let’s be honest… We’ve all heard this everywhere: “Your data is secured with AES-256 encryption” Cool… but what does that even mean? I used to just nod like: “Yeah yeah… sounds secure 😌” But...

item.title: I Built an OPA Plugin That Turns It Into an AuthZEN-Compatible PDP
item.description: Introduction In my previous article, I did a deep dive into the AuthZEN Authorization API 1.0 spec. It standardizes communication between PEPs and PDPs. You send a JSON request asking "can this...

item.title: 📦 Monorepo Architecture with pnpm Workspace, Turborepo & Changesets
item.description: When you're developing a project with multiple packages, managing each one in its own repo can quickly turn into a nightmare. In this article, we'll set up a monorepo architecture from scratch...

item.title: Reimplementing COMMAND.COM in Go for Unix-like Platforms: Challenges and Solutions
item.description: Introduction Reimplementing COMMAND.COM in Go for Unix-like platforms is more than a nostalgic exercise—it’s a technical bridge between legacy DOS functionality and modern systems. At its core,...

item.description: Originally published at kjetilfuras.com Rich link preview cards make a real difference on Bluesky—the clickable previews that show a title, description, and image when you share a URL. Same thing...

item.title: LiteLLM vs AegisFlow: honest comparison from someone who built the alternative
item.description: I built AegisFlow because I needed an LLM proxy and kept running into things with LiteLLM that didn't work for me. This isn't a takedown. LiteLLM has a huge community, 100+ providers, and a lot of...

item.title: Is “Hackback” Official US Cybersecurity Strategy?
item.description: The 2026 US “Cyber Strategy for America” document is mostly the same thing we’ve seen out of the White House for over a decade, but with a more aggressive tone. But one sentence stood out: “We...

item.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
I tested a living room full of cheap Ikea speakers against Sonos and Bose
item.description: Ikea's $10 Kallsup Bluetooth speaker is fun and colorful and sounds better than its price suggests. It's not mind-blowing (it is a $10 speaker, after all), and there are no features to speak of,...

item.title: Snapchat’s ‘Reals’ joke mocks Instagram’s many ripoffs

item.title: Cognichip wants AI to design the chips that power AI, and just raised $60M to try
item.description: The firm says it can can reduce the cost of chip development by more than 75% and cut the timeline by more than half.

item.description: What's expected to be the biggest IPO in human history is reportedly underway. SpaceX has filed for an initial public offering with the Securities and Exchange Commission, according to a report...

item.title: These Raspberry Pi price hikes are no joke
item.description: As of today, the price of the 16GB version of the Raspberry Pi 5 is going up by $100, a price bump that's almost as much as the original $120 price tag. Driven by the ongoing RAM shortage,...

item.title: The best Amazon Big Spring Sale deals you can still get
item.description: Amazon’s Big Spring Sale is technically over, but not all of the deals are gone. In fact, dozens of deals are still here and selling at the same prices, while other discounts aren’t quite as low...

item.title: The Korg Handytraxx Play finally got me learning to scratch
item.description: About 20 years ago, I bought a turntable with the idea that it would be a staple of my music-making setup. I dreamed of learning to scratch and digging through dollar bin vinyl for samples....

item.title: Microsoft Warns of WhatsApp-Delivered VBS Malware Hijacking Windows via UAC Bypass
item.description: Microsoft is calling attention to a new campaign that has leveraged WhatsApp messages to distribute malicious Visual Basic Script (VBS) files. The activity, beginning in late February 2026,...

item.title: Less than a month: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
item.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together on April 30. Space is limited. Register here for your pass.

item.title: A YouTuber channeled his distaste for the PS5’s design into slick console covers
item.description: The PlayStation 5's curvy design is divisive. The Verge's Andrew Webster called it a "robotic clam" in his 2020 review, which is actually kind of endearing. Personally, I kind of got over hating...

item.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
item.description: Google partnered with the Brazilian government on a satellite imagery map to help protect the country’s forests.

item.title: Block the Prompt, Not the Work: The End of "Doctor No"
item.description: There is a character that keeps appearing in enterprise security departments, and most CISOs know exactly who that is. It doesn’t build. It doesn’t enable. Its entire function is to say "No." No...

item.title: Casbaneiro Phishing Targets Latin America and Europe Using Dynamic PDF Lures
item.description: A multi-pronged phishing campaign is targeting Spanish-speaking users in organizations across Latin America and Europe to deliver Windows banking trojans like Casbaneiro (aka Metamorfo) via...

item.title: The Download: gig workers training humanoids, and better AI benchmarks
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The gig workers who are training humanoid robots at home...
